Using the accusation-scene anchor from the instructions together with the frames from this bounded clip, the only visually grounded option for the accused person is the person with the blonde wig.

trace_inference_steps.text: The relevant event remains the forest confrontation around 149.82s-162.468s, where the visible speaker anchor is a man in a light gray jacket holding a lantern.

Both transcript spans available for this moment contain only calls to "Will" / "Will?" and do not include the accusation wording or the word "kidnapper," so the accusation itself is still not textually grounded.

The frames do show another visible person with long blonde hair, possibly a blonde wig, near the man in the light gray jacket during this confrontation, but without the accusation wording there is no grounded basis to conclude that this visible person is the one being accused.

Because the new evidence still does not resolve the accusation/denial exchange or tie the accusation target to one visible person at that exact moment, none of the answer options can be selected with confidence from the provided evidence alone.
